proofs.control: Recaps are generated from settled data only

evidence.window_gate: recap-service.ts:166 — settlement.created_at filtered by time window

proofs.notes: Recap generation has a triple filter: (1) status must be "settled", (2) result must be win/loss/push, (3) created_at within recap window. Verified in code at recap-service.ts lines 153-167. 43 settlement records in DB all have valid statuses.

proofs.control: No derived metrics use stale or non-canonical data

evidence.data_source: settlement_records table (canonical) → joined to picks table
evidence.code_path: recap-service.ts:152 — repositories.settlements.listRecent() → fresh DB query every time
evidence.freshness: Every recap run queries live DB — no cached aggregates

proofs.notes: Recaps query settlement_records directly via repositories.settlements.listRecent() — no cache, no materialized views, no stale snapshots. Every run hits live DB. 0 temporal violations found in 40 picks.

evidence.methodology: Independent recomputation from raw settlement_records + picks tables

proofs.notes: Independent cross-check from raw DB: 24-10-0 record, 14.42 net units, 42.41% ROI across 40 settled picks. Formula matches recap-service.ts implementation.

evidence.standard_compliance: Standard American odds ROI formula — matches industry convention
edge_cases: null odds excluded from profit calculation

proofs.notes: ROI formula at recap-service.ts:217-220 is standard: (netUnits / totalRiskedUnits) * 100. Profit uses American odds conversion. Division-by-zero guard present. Null stake defaults to 1 unit. Matches industry convention.
